notes.note: A Greek-named dish of salt-fish and cheese; a homely delicacy Cicero relishes here as a gift mentioned in Atticus's letter.

notes.anchor: Antium is to Rome what your Buthrotum is to Corcyra
notes.note: Antium (modern Anzio) was Cicero's favourite quiet seaside retreat from Rome, paired here with Atticus's villa estate at Buthrotum in Epirus, near the island of Corcyra (Corfu).
A learned grammarian from Amisus, brought to Rome as a prisoner of war; he catalogued and arranged Cicero's library, with help from Atticus's slaves Dionysius and Menophilus.
Latin sillybi (Greek sittybai): the small parchment tags hung from a papyrus roll bearing its title, here added to brighten the newly shelved books.
Atticus owned and let out a troupe of gladiators; Cicero, half in jest, asks for news of them only if they are fighting well.
